lesson: A LID OPEN alarm with the lid physically shut comes down to the interlock: if the hall sensor reads zero field, the lid magnet is gone entirely — realigning what isn't there won't help, and it's an $8 part, not the board.
symptoms: LID OPEN alarm at every startup.

tests.error-log: An interlock fault latched since before open — the controller refuses to start until it clears.
tests.inspect-beater: The hopper lid has seen better days — worn fittings, tired seal face. Hard to say if it's making clean contact all round.
tests.continuity-test: The lid's hall-effect sensor reads zero field with the lid closed.

flavour: Eight dollars of magnet was holding the entire operation hostage.
authenticityNote: Hopper lid magnets are small press-fit items that fall out if the lid is dropped or slammed. The hall sensor reads no field and locks the machine out — frequently misdiagnosed as a board fault.
